implementation_steps.description: Inspect the base formatter class to understand the interface that YamlFormatter must implement
Study the JSON formatter implementation as a reference for handling nested data structures
Review CSV formatter to understand the full formatter interface and error handling patterns
Check how formatters are registered and exposed to understand integration points
Examine how the --format parameter is handled and how formatters are instantiated
Review CLI argument parsing to understand how format options are defined
Add pyyaml dependency to the requirements file
Create YamlFormatter class implementing the base formatter interface with proper nested data structure handling, including custom YAML dumping options for readability
Import and register the new YamlFormatter to make it available to the CLI
Create comprehensive unit tests for YamlFormatter covering nested objects, arrays, edge cases, and error conditions
Add integration tests to verify YAML format works with existing format selection mechanism
Verify if any configuration changes are needed to support YAML format options
Add redis dependency with version specification (e.g., redis>=4.0.0)
Create abstract base class defining cache interface with methods: get(), set(), delete(), clear(), exists(). Include TTL support and serialization hooks.
Refactor existing memory cache to inherit from base_cache.BaseCache and implement the standardized interface
Implement RedisCache class inheriting from BaseCache. Include connection management, error handling, serialization (pickle/json), TTL support, and graceful degradation on connection failures.
Create factory class to instantiate appropriate cache backend based on configuration. Include fallback logic from Redis to memory cache on connection failure.
Add Redis configuration defaults: redis_enabled=False, redis_host='localhost', redis_port=6379, redis_db=0, cache_ttl=3600, cache_backend='memory'
Add Redis configuration section with host, port, db, enabled flag, TTL settings, and cache backend selection
Update configuration loading to handle Redis settings, validate Redis connection parameters, and provide cache configuration validation
Modify pipeline to use cache factory instead of direct memory cache instantiation. Update cache key generation strategy and integrate TTL support.
Add pytest fixtures for mocked Redis client, cache factory configuration, and test cache instances for both memory and Redis backends
Create comprehensive tests for both memory and Redis cache implementations using mocks. Test error scenarios, TTL behavior, serialization, and fallback mechanisms.
Test cache factory functionality including backend selection, fallback behavior, configuration handling, and Redis connection failure scenarios using mocks
Update existing pipeline tests to work with new cache abstraction. Add tests for pipeline caching with different backends and cache miss/hit scenarios.
Create dry-run context manager and utilities module with DryRunContext class, global state management, decorators for dry-run aware functions, and summary collection utilities
Add dry_run parameter support to configuration management, ensure it can be passed through the system and accessed by all components
Add --dry-run flag to main CLI parser, initialize dry-run context, and ensure the flag is passed to all command handlers
Modify process command to accept and handle dry_run parameter, pass it to pipeline execution, and show dry-run messaging
Update validate command to support dry_run mode, skip actual validation writes while showing what would be validated
Add dry_run support to inspect command, showing what files would be inspected without performing expensive operations
Make file I/O operations dry-run aware by adding dry_run checks before actual file writes, logging intended operations instead
Integrate dry-run support into pipeline execution, pass dry_run context to all transforms and output formatters
Update transform classes to be dry-run aware, logging what transformations would be applied without modifying data
Add dry_run support to base output format class, providing interface for dry-run behavior in formatters
Implement dry-run behavior for CSV output, showing intended file writes and format details without creating files
Implement dry-run behavior for JSON output, displaying intended output structure without writing files
Add dry-run specific test fixtures and helper functions for testing dry-run behavior across the system
Create comprehensive tests for dry-run functionality including context management, flag propagation, and summary generation
Add dry-run test cases for all command modules, ensuring proper flag handling and dry-run behavior
Update pipeline tests to include dry-run scenarios, verifying transforms and outputs behave correctly in dry-run mode
Add dry-run specific tests for output formatters, ensuring no files are created and proper logging occurs
